title: Fix HTMX polling destroying Alpine.js state on orchestrators panel
analysis:
The dashboard uses HTMX polling (every 10s/15s) with hx-swap="innerHTML" on three sections in index.html: memory, recon, and orchestrators. The orchestrators partial (orchestrators.html) uses Alpine.js x-data="{ open: false }" for show/hide task accordions. Each HTMX innerHTML swap destroys the entire DOM subtree and re-creates it, resetting all Alpine.js component state — so expanded accordions collapse every poll cycle.

The fix uses the idiomorph extension for HTMX, which performs a DOM morph (diff) instead of a full innerHTML replace. This preserves Alpine.js component state because existing DOM nodes are patched in-place rather than destroyed and recreated.

Implementation: (1) Add the idiomorph extension script to base.html with a pinned version, (2) Add hx-ext="morph" to the body tag so the extension is available to all HTMX elements, (3) Change all three section hx-swap attributes from "innerHTML" to "morph:innerHTML" in index.html.

Existing tests in test_app.py (TestIndex class) only check for hx-get attributes, not hx-swap, so they will continue to pass. The orchestrator panel tests in test_orchestrator_panel.py test the partial content, not the swap mechanism, so they are also unaffected.

The test strategy uses the same pattern as existing tests: render the full index page via GET / and assert on the HTML content for the new idiomorph-related attributes.

steps.description: Write failing tests in test_app.py in a new TestIdiomorphExtension class: (1) test_idiomorph_script_tag — GET / and assert the rendered HTML contains the idiomorph extension script URL 'unpkg.com/idiomorph@0.3.0/dist/idiomorph-ext.min.js', (2) test_body_has_morph_extension — GET / and assert 'hx-ext="morph"' appears in the HTML. Both use the existing client fixture.

Modify base.html: (1) Add <script src="https://unpkg.com/idiomorph@0.3.0/dist/idiomorph-ext.min.js"></script> after the htmx script tag (after line 8), (2) Change <body> tag (line 12) to <body class="h-full bg-gray-900 text-gray-100" hx-ext="morph">. This makes the idiomorph morph swap strategy available to all HTMX elements.

Write failing tests in test_app.py in a new TestMorphSwap class: (1) test_memory_section_uses_morph — assert 'hx-swap="morph:innerHTML"' appears near hx-get="/partials/memory", (2) test_recon_section_uses_morph — same for /partials/recon, (3) test_orchestrators_section_uses_morph — same for /partials/orchestrators, (4) test_no_plain_innerhtml_on_polling_sections — assert the plain 'hx-swap="innerHTML"' string does NOT appear in the rendered index page, confirming all sections were migrated.
Modify index.html: Change all three hx-swap="innerHTML" attributes to hx-swap="morph:innerHTML" — on the memory section (line 9), recon section (line 18), and orchestrators section (line 27).

design_decisions.decision: Use idiomorph extension (option 1 from task) rather than localStorage state or selective swapping
design_decisions.rationale: Idiomorph is the simplest, most maintainable approach — a single script tag + attribute change. It handles all Alpine.js state preservation automatically via DOM morphing. No custom JavaScript needed, no state serialization/restoration logic. The HTMX maintainers recommend this pattern for Alpine.js compatibility.

design_decisions.decision: Pin idiomorph to version 0.3.0 via unpkg CDN
design_decisions.rationale: Version pinning prevents unexpected breakage from upstream changes. Using unpkg is consistent with the existing pattern of loading htmx and Alpine.js from CDNs (lines 8-9 of base.html). Version 0.3.0 is stable and compatible with htmx 2.x.

design_decisions.decision: Add hx-ext="morph" to the <body> tag rather than to each individual section
design_decisions.rationale: Placing the extension attribute on body makes the morph strategy available to all HTMX elements without repeating the attribute. This is cleaner and follows the HTMX documentation recommendation for page-wide extensions.

design_decisions.decision: Apply morph:innerHTML to all three polling sections, not just orchestrators
design_decisions.rationale: Task explicitly requests consistency across all sections. Even though memory and recon don't currently use Alpine.js, applying morph:innerHTML prevents future state-destruction bugs if Alpine or other stateful JS is added to those panels.

design_decisions.decision: Add tests to existing test_app.py rather than creating a new test file
design_decisions.rationale: The existing TestIndex class in test_app.py already tests the rendered index page HTML attributes (hx-get, content type, etc.). Adding new test classes in the same file keeps related tests co-located and follows the established pattern.

reuse.how: Reuse the existing 'client' fixture to make GET / requests and inspect the rendered HTML, same pattern used by TestIndex class.
Follow the same pattern: client.get('/').text then assert substring presence in HTML. This is the established convention for testing template output.
